title: Queue-age badge & oldest-first sort compute from case-creation time, not time-since-submitted-for-review
expected: The fresh/warning/overdue badge and the oldest-first ordering reflect how long a case has been WAITING IN THE ATTORNEY QUEUE (time since it was submitted for review), so the SLA signal is honest.
got: Both the review page and the dashboard map `submittedAt: c.createdAt` (review/page.tsx:35, dashboard/page.tsx:37). `StoredCase.createdAt` is the case-creation timestamp ('exposed as queue age proxy', petitions.ts:39; types.ts:79-80), set once at createCaseWithCriteria (pglite-store.ts:374-388). transitionCase into 'Attorney Review' updates only status + updated_at, NEVER created_at (pglite-store.ts:501). getCasesInReview also orders by created_at asc (pglite-store.ts:433-441). Net: a case created 9 days ago but submitted to the queue an hour ago shows a red '9d overdue' badge and sorts to the top, despite the attorney having had it for 1 hour; a recently-created case reads 'fresh' regardless of queue wait. The age machinery (ageBucket/formatAge/sortOldestFirst) is itself correct and well-tested (queue-age.ts:26-74) — only the input timestamp is wrong. A better source already exists: the case_reviews 'submitted' event carries its own created_at (reviews.ts:35, appended in the same transition, pglite-store.ts:508), and cases.updated_at is bumped on the transition (just not exposed; CASE_COLUMNS omits it, pglite-store.ts:186).
suggested_acceptance: The queue-age badge and oldest-first sort derive from the time the case was submitted for review (the case_reviews 'submitted' event timestamp) or cases.updated_at — not created_at. A case submitted an hour ago reads 'fresh' regardless of how old the case file is; a case sitting in the attorney queue past 24h reads 'overdue'.
l2_priority: Live: create a case, age it (or backdate created_at), submit it fresh, and confirm the queue badge reflects QUEUE wait time, not file age. Conversely confirm a long-queued case escalates to overdue. This is the core SLA-honesty assertion for the ops persona.

title: No ops/read-only role — a non-attorney case manager cannot even VIEW the SLA review queue
expected: A legal-ops / case manager can WATCH the review queue (age/staleness badges, oldest-first) to manage SLAs, without being granted attorney sign/file power.
got: The codebase has exactly two roles: owner (applicant) and attorney (isConfiguredAttorney, roles.ts:40). The review queue AND the dashboard nav link to it both gate on isConfiguredAttorney (review/page.tsx:16-17, DashboardView.tsx:35). A non-attorney is shown 'Your account isn't on the attorney allowlist' with an empty body (ReviewQueueView.tsx:71-79). The only way to see the queue is to be added to ATTORNEY_EMAILS — which simultaneously hands the user sign/file power they should never hold. There is no read-only observer tier. Per the character brief, the inability to VIEW (as opposed to ACT on) the queue is a finding for her job. Distinct from the accepted 'Production attorney RBAC' two-tier fail-closed posture (which correctly walls off ACTIONS and is a security strength) — this is the absence of a third, read-only tier.
suggested_acceptance: A non-attorney 'ops/case-manager' role (or a read-only flag) can open /dashboard/review and see the queue + age badges, but cannot request-changes / sign / file. Viewing is decoupled from acting.
l2_priority: Live: confirm whether any role short of the attorney allowlist can reach the queue; if not, confirm the magnitude of the job-impact for the ops persona (she cannot do her primary SLA-watching task without either lockout or over-privilege).

title: No aging / staleness signal on the real-case dashboard board — a stalled matter is invisible without opening it
expected: From the dashboard board I can see WHICH matters are aging/overdue at a glance (e.g. days-in-current-stage), so a case stalled between stages is visible without opening it.
got: The real-case board (YourCasesCard) renders each case with fileNumber, petitioner, classification, a status badge, and likelihood — but NO elapsed-time or staleness indicator (CaseFileDashboard.tsx:136-172). A case stalled in Drafting for 9 days looks identical to one created an hour ago. The age badge logic exists only on /dashboard/review (which I usually can't reach). The one board WITH ops affordances (search/status filter/sort/CSV/'Target file' column, CaseList.tsx) is wired to the MOCK demo portfolio (getCases() -> lib/data/cases.ts, an accepted gap), not my live caseload — so the powerful board is fake data and my real cases get a flat list with no aging.
suggested_acceptance: The real-case board surfaces a per-case age / days-in-current-stage indicator (reusing the queue-age helper once it reads the right timestamp), so an aging matter is visible from the board without opening it.
l2_priority: Live: confirm the real-case board shows no aging signal and judge how much of the ops triage win is lost (she must open each case or hand-track to find the stalled one).

title: Roadmap stepper marks 'Attorney review' as the CURRENT stage while the case status is still Drafting / un-submitted
expected: The roadmap's current stage matches what actually happened: a case that has NOT been submitted for review should not show 'Attorney review' as the active/current stage.
got: caseRoadmap's pre-submission branch sets current = REVIEW (the 'Attorney review' stage) as soon as a draft exists, while status is still 'Drafting' and nothing has been submitted (roadmap.ts:51; roadmap.test.ts:28 names this 'ready to submit'). The stepper renders that stage with the active dot/label (RoadmapStepper.tsx:31-47). Triaging fast off the stepper, a reader can misread 'Attorney review · current' as 'it's with the attorney' when it's actually parked in Drafting with no one accountable. Mitigated: the case-detail status Fact ('Drafting') is shown directly above (CaseDetailView.tsx:121), so it's an ambiguity, not a lie — hence confusion/minor, not a trust blocker.
suggested_acceptance: When a draft exists but the case is still in Drafting (un-submitted), the 'Attorney review' stage reads 'upcoming' (or a distinct 'ready to submit' state), not 'current' — so the stepper doesn't imply the attorney already has it.
l2_priority: Live: confirm whether the 'current' styling on Attorney-review-while-Drafting is read as 'submitted' by an ops user, or whether the adjacent status Fact resolves it.

title: Statuses are true and lifecycle transitions are atomic — no case shown in a stage it isn't in
expected: The board's statuses reflect reality and can't be faked or desynced.
got: Status comes straight from the persisted column; every transition is an atomic compare-and-set that flips status and appends the review-log events in one transaction (transitionCase, pglite-store.ts:480-525), so a stale tab / double-submit can't double-file or land a case in an impossible stage, and the audit log never desyncs from status. Criterion 1 (correct, current statuses) holds.
suggested_acceptance: Keep the atomic compare-and-set lifecycle; it is the honest core of the board.

title: Queue is oldest-first at DB + view, null-safe, refreshed, aria-labeled; cross-tenant read fail-closed
expected: The queue ordering and badge machinery are sound, and the queue can't be used to enumerate other applicants' cases.
got: getCasesInReview orders by created_at asc and the view re-sorts oldest-first (pglite-store.ts:433, ReviewQueueView.tsx:44). ageBucket/formatAge are null-safe and reject future timestamps, the clock refreshes every 60s, and the badge carries an aria-label (queue-age.ts:26-60, ReviewQueueView.tsx:99-120). The queue is gated by isConfiguredAttorney (fail-closed) so it can't enumerate other applicants' cases/IDs (review/page.tsx:8-17). Only the input timestamp (tv-attorney-01) is wrong — the sort/bucket logic itself is solid.
suggested_acceptance: Keep the oldest-first + null-safe + fail-closed queue machinery; only re-point the age timestamp (tv-attorney-01) and add a read-only tier (tv-attorney-02).

title: Evidence categorization is well-grounded and movement is visible on the board
expected: Adding evidence is grounded in the real document and shows up as case movement, so a case can't silently stall on evidence.
got: The categorize prompt receives the real document name + pasted text, the case's classification pack criteria, and a read-only summary of existing vault buckets (route.ts:79-80, buildCategorizePrompt, evidence.ts:106-143); coverage/gaps derive from the real persisted docs (summarizeVault, evidence.ts:226); exhibits are monotonic and never reused (pglite-store.ts:646-664); DISCLAIMER rides every result (evidence.ts:205). Once a doc exists, hasEvidence flips and the roadmap advances the Evidence stage to done (CaseDetailView.tsx:135, roadmap.ts:51) — so evidence movement is visible on the board. Criterion 7 (disclaimer on AI work product) holds.
suggested_acceptance: Keep the grounded categorize prompt (real text + pack + sibling buckets) and the roadmap's live derivation of evidence presence.
l2_priority: Live (covered by paralegal/researcher): confirm categorization accuracy on real docs; for ops, confirm evidence addition is visibly reflected as case movement.
